task: I need a quick way to sanity-check a report before I send it out to a client. Can you help me build a simple pre-send checklist so I don’t miss anything obvious or hand over something sloppy?

prefs.explanation: She would likely prefer responses that get to the point quickly and avoid padding. Her persona is highly pragmatic, so extra prose would read as inefficiency rather than helpfulness. She still wants enough detail to act, but not a long-winded explanation.

She seems to value straightforward answers over hedging or polite cushioning. In her world, vague language wastes time and can hide the real issue. She would probably respond best to clear conclusions, explicit risks, and no unnecessary reassurance.

She likely prefers information laid out in a compact, actionable format rather than flowing prose. Structured lists fit her control-oriented style and make it easier to compare options or spot gaps. Freeform discussion is less useful to her unless she specifically asks for it.

She probably wants the assistant to work with her, not take over and not just narrate options endlessly. She is likely to value fast back-and-forth when refining a plan, especially if it preserves her control over the final decision. Overly prescriptive help could feel intrusive.

prefs.short: Wants hard pushback on bad moves
prefs.explanation: She seems likely to respect an assistant that flags weak assumptions, rushed decisions, or hidden downside. Because she treats life as a strategic contest, she would probably see useful pushback as part of good help rather than as rudeness. Soft agreement without scrutiny would be less valuable to her.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, fast pre-send checklist you can run through before sending a report to a client.

## Quick Pre-Send Report Checklist

### 1) Client and document basics
- [ ] Correct client name everywhere
- [ ] Correct project/report title
- [ ] Right date/version on the document
- [ ] Recipient list is correct
- [ ] Confidentiality or disclaimer text included if needed

### 2) Content sanity check
- [ ] Main takeaway is clear
- [ ] Report answers the client’s question/request
- [ ] No obvious gaps or missing sections
- [ ] Recommendations are specific and actionable
- [ ] Claims are supported by data or evidence

### 3) Numbers and facts
- [ ] All figures add up correctly
- [ ] Percentages, totals, and units are consistent
- [ ] Dates, names, and references are accurate
- [ ] No placeholder text left in the report
- [ ] Sources are cited where needed

### 4) Formatting and presentation
- [ ] Headings are consistent
- [ ] Tables/charts are readable and labeled
- [ ] Fonts, spacing, and alignment look clean
- [ ] Page numbers are correct
- [ ] File name is clear and professional

### 5) Writing quality
- [ ] No typos or grammar mistakes
- [ ] Tone is professional and client-appropriate
- [ ] Sentences are clear and concise
- [ ] No jargon unless the client expects it
- [ ] No accidental repetition

### 6) Final send check
- [ ] Correct file attached
- [ ] Attachment opens properly
- [ ] Email/message references the report correctly
- [ ] Any promised next steps are included
- [ ] You’ve done one final skim from start to finish

## Super-short version
If you only have 2 minutes, check:
1. Correct client/report name  
2. Numbers and dates are right  
3. No typos or placeholders  
4. Charts/tables look clean  
5. File attached is the final version  

If you want, I can turn this into a one-page checklist you can copy into Word, Notion, or a team template.

per_pref.reason: Very concise and practical: “simple, fast pre-send checklist” and a compact bullet list with a “Super-short version.” No wasted prose.

Direct and straightforward throughout, e.g. “Correct client name everywhere,” “No typos or grammar mistakes,” and “If you only have 2 minutes, check...” It is clear, though not especially blunt or pushback-heavy.
Strongly formatted as bullets and checklists with numbered sections and checkboxes, exactly matching the preference for structured, actionable layout.
Collaborative and efficient: it gives a usable checklist and offers refinement with “If you want, I can turn this into a one-page checklist...” without overexplaining or taking over.
There is little hard pushback. It helps sanity-check, but it doesn’t actively flag risky assumptions or challenge weak moves; the tone is mostly agreeable and supportive rather than critical.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, client-work version you can actually use.

## 10-second version
**Don’t send if any of these are true:**
- [ ] Missing source docs / inputs
- [ ] Math not checked against source file
- [ ] Client name / project code / report title wrong
- [ ] I can’t defend every claim if challenged
- [ ] Attachment, version number, and date not verified
- [ ] Final read shows tone issues, gaps, or sloppy wording

## Full version
**Pre-send hard stop checklist**

### 1) Source and inputs
- [ ] All source docs received and reviewed
- [ ] No missing inputs, assumptions, or dependencies
- [ ] Any gaps are flagged clearly in the report
- [ ] Source file names / references match what was used

### 2) Numbers and logic
- [ ] Key figures checked against source file
- [ ] Math, totals, percentages, and units verified
- [ ] No copy/paste errors in tables or charts
- [ ] Any estimates or judgments are labeled as such
- [ ] I can explain how every key number was derived

### 3) Client/admin details
- [ ] Client name is correct everywhere
- [ ] Project code / job number is correct
- [ ] Report title is correct
- [ ] Date is correct
- [ ] Version number is correct
- [ ] Recipient list is correct

### 4) Defensibility
- [ ] Every major claim is supported by a source, note, or calculation
- [ ] No statement I’d hesitate to defend if challenged
- [ ] Recommendations are grounded in the evidence
- [ ] Risks, caveats, and limitations are stated plainly
- [ ] No overclaiming, hedging, or unsupported certainty

### 5) Final read
- [ ] One clean read-through from start to finish
- [ ] Tone is professional and client-appropriate
- [ ] No obvious gaps, contradictions, or missing context
- [ ] No awkward wording, typos, or formatting errors
- [ ] Attachments are correct and open properly

## Copy-paste version
You can paste this into Notes, Word, or your task system:

**PRE-SEND CHECK**
- [ ] Source docs complete
- [ ] Math checked against source file
- [ ] Client name correct
- [ ] Project code correct
- [ ] Report title correct
- [ ] Date/version correct
- [ ] I can defend every key claim
- [ ] Attachments correct
- [ ] Final read done for tone/gaps/sloppiness

If you want, I can make this even more practical as:
1. a **red/yellow/green go-no-go version**, or  
2. a **two-column checklist with “stop if failed” wording**.

per_pref.reason: Very concise and efficient: opens with “here’s a tighter” and gives a compact “10-second version” plus a short copy-paste checklist.
Direct and blunt: uses hard-stop language like “Don’t send if any of these are true” and “No statement I’d hesitate to defend if challenged,” with little hedging.
Strong bullet/checklist structure throughout, including nested sections and a final copy-paste checklist.
Collaborative and efficient: provides a usable checklist and offers two optional refinements (“red/yellow/green” or “two-column checklist”) without taking over.
Clear pushback on bad moves: explicitly flags unsupported claims, overclaiming, missing inputs, and says “Don’t send if any of these are true.”

turns.agent_response:
Got it. Here’s a more operational version.

## 10-second go/no-go
**STOP if any of these are true:**
- Missing/incomplete source docs
- Math not tied back to source file
- Client/admin details wrong
- Can’t defend a claim if challenged
- Attachment/version/date not verified
- Final read not done for tone/gaps/overreach

## Full version
**STOP NOW**
- [ ] Source docs missing or incomplete
- [ ] Math not tied back to source file
- [ ] Client name / project code / version / date / attachment not verified
- [ ] Anything in here I can’t defend if challenged

**CHECK IF TIME**
- [ ] Final pass for tone
- [ ] Obvious gaps or missing context
- [ ] Any overclaiming / sloppy wording
- [ ] Attachment opens and is the right file

## Copy-paste version
**PRE-SEND**
STOP NOW:
- [ ] Source docs missing/incomplete
- [ ] Math not tied to source file
- [ ] Client name / project code / version / date / attachment wrong or unverified
- [ ] Can’t defend every claim if challenged

CHECK IF TIME:
- [ ] Final read for tone
- [ ] Gaps / missing context / overreach
- [ ] Attachment is correct and opens

If you want, I can make this even more stripped down into a **single-line yes/no gate** you can paste at the top of every report.

per_pref.reason: Very concise and efficient: starts with “Got it. Here’s a more operational version,” then gives compact checklists with little padding.
Direct and blunt: uses “STOP if any of these are true,” “STOP NOW,” and “Can’t defend a claim if challenged,” with no softening.
Strongly formatted as bullets and checklists, including “10-second go/no-go,” “Full version,” and “Copy-paste version.”
Collaborative and efficient: provides a usable checklist and offers refinement with “If you want, I can make this even more stripped down,” without taking over.
Includes useful pushback criteria like “Can’t defend a claim if challenged” and flags “overclaiming / sloppy wording,” but it doesn’t deeply challenge assumptions beyond basic sanity checks.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the uglier, more usable version.

## 10-second go/no-go
**YES / NO: Can I send this without stopping on source docs, math, admin details, defensibility, attachment, or final read?**

## Copy-paste gate

**STOP NOW**
- [ ] Source docs missing or incomplete
- [ ] Math not tied back to source file
- [ ] Client name / project code / version / date / attachment not verified
- [ ] Can’t defend this if challenged

**CHECK IF TIME**
- [ ] Final read for tone / gaps / overreach
- [ ] Attachment is the right file and opens

If you want, I can make this even more brutal as a one-line checklist you can paste at the top of every report note.

per_pref.reason: Very concise and no padding: "here’s the uglier, more usable version" and a short checklist with minimal prose.
Blunt and unsoftened: uses "STOP NOW," "YES / NO," and "Can’t defend this if challenged" instead of hedging.
Strong bullet/checklist format throughout, with compact actionable items under "Copy-paste gate".
Collaborative and efficient: offers a usable checklist and says "If you want, I can make this even more brutal," but doesn’t over-narrate options.
Includes some pushback on weak work via "Can’t defend this if challenged" and "STOP NOW," though it doesn’t deeply challenge assumptions.
